formulas.name: Quadratic Formula
formulas.description: Used to find the roots of a quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0.

formulas.name: Euler's Identity
formulas.description: A beautiful equation connecting five fundamental mathematical constants.
formulas.example: Used in complex analysis and physics.

formulas.name: Pythagorean Theorem
formulas.description: Relates the sides of a right-angled triangle.
formulas.example: If a=3 and b=4, then c=5.

formulas.name: Area of a Circle
formulas.description: Calculates the area enclosed by a circle with radius r.
formulas.example: If r=7, A = 49\pi.

formulas.name: Binomial Theorem
formulas.description: Describes the algebraic expansion of powers of a binomial.

formulas.name: Logarithm Product Rule
formulas.description: The logarithm of a product is the sum of the logarithms.

formulas.name: Derivative Power Rule
formulas.description: Basic rule for differentiating powers of x.
formulas.example: The derivative of x^3 is 3x^2.

formulas.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
formulas.description: Relates differentiation and integration.
formulas.example: Used to calculate the area under a curve.

formulas.name: Law of Sines
formulas.description: Relates the lengths of the sides of a triangle to the sines of its angles.
formulas.example: Used to solve non-right triangles.

formulas.name: Law of Cosines
formulas.description: Generalization of the Pythagorean theorem for all triangles.
formulas.example: Find side c if a, b and angle C are known.

formulas.name: Bayes' Theorem
formulas.description: Describes the probability of an event based on prior knowledge.
formulas.example: Used in medical testing and spam filters.

formulas.name: Standard Deviation
formulas.description: Measure of the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values.
formulas.example: High sigma means data is spread out.

formulas.name: Newton's Second Law
formulas.description: Force equals mass times acceleration.
formulas.example: A 10kg mass accelerating at 2m/s^2 requires 20N of force.

formulas.name: Mass-Energy Equivalence
formulas.description: Einstein's famous equation relating mass and energy.
formulas.example: Shows that mass can be converted into energy.

formulas.name: Ohm's Law
formulas.description: Relates voltage, current, and resistance.
formulas.example: If I=2A and R=5Î©, then V=10V.

formulas.name: Volume of a Sphere
formulas.description: Calculates the space occupied by a sphere.
formulas.example: If r=3, V = 36\pi.

formulas.name: Arithmetic Series Sum
formulas.description: Sum of the first n terms of an arithmetic progression.
formulas.example: Sum of 1 to 100 is 5050.

formulas.name: Product Rule
formulas.description: Rule for differentiating the product of two functions.
formulas.example: Derivative of x*sin(x) is sin(x) + x*cos(x).

formulas.name: Pythagorean Identity
formulas.description: Fundamental identity relating sine and cosine.
formulas.example: True for any angle theta.

formulas.name: Combinations Formula
formulas.description: Number of ways to choose k items from n without regard to order.
formulas.example: Choosing 2 items from 5: 10 ways.
